avoid: Ignoring correctness failures for performance gains
Hardcoding hidden dimensions (d) or tensor sizes
Using scalar (vector_width=1) loads for large contiguous memory regions
Promoting any candidate that fails correctness on all tested shapes
evidence: Candidate failed correctness for all three shapes (3584, 4096, 4608)
Max absolute error is 5.08 at d=4608, far above acceptable tolerance
Relative L2 error ~0.00029 exceeds strict floating-point error bound
Static review and compilation passed, implying bug is in kernel logic, not interface
No benchmark data available, but correctness failure dominates

recommended_actions: check rank-16 indexing and vector alignment
Debug the custom rank-16 update kernel: verify indexing, boundary conditions, and shared memory usage
Use vectorized loads (float4) to improve memory coalescing and reduce instruction count
Test with a simple reference kernel (e.g., naive for-loop) for each shape to isolate the bug
Consider implementing the rank-16 update via a small GEMM (cuBLAS) to eliminate custom kernel risk
Add shape-aware handling if kernel assumes block-size alignment that fails for non-multiple dimensions
